title.en: One-eyed Beast of a Hundred Voices

summary.zh: 翼望之山的怪兽，形如野猫，一目三尾，叫声仿佛百声并出；畜之可御凶邪，佩之能治黄疸。
summary.en: A wildcat-like beast of Mount Yiwang with a single eye and three tails, its cry like a hundred voices at once; it wards off evil, and worn on the body it cures jaundice.

description.zh:
翼望之山是西次三经的最后一座山，“无草木，多金玉”。山中有兽“其状如狸，一目而三尾，名曰讙，其音如夺百声”——形如野猫，只生一目，身后三条尾巴，叫起来仿佛上百种声音齐出，一说它的声音能压过百声。经文接着记它的用处：“是可以御凶，服之已瘅”——畜养它可以抵御凶邪，佩带它能治愈黄疸（瘅）。

“御凶”是西山经诸兽常见的功效，同经的天狗、鵸鵌皆有此能，讙则兼具医效，一身而三奇：独目、三尾、百声。这也使它成为明清《山海经》图本中辨识度最高的小兽之一，图工通常画一只竖着三条尾巴、额头正中生独眼的猫形兽。与它同住翼望之山的，是三首六尾而善笑的怪鸟鵸鵌——西次三经的末山，集中了全经造形最奇的两只小动物。
description.en:
Mount Yiwang, last peak of the third western guideway, 'bears no plants, but much gold and jade'. Its beast is the Huan: 'shaped like a wildcat, with one eye and three tails, its voice as if seizing a hundred voices' — a cry in which a hundred animal calls seem to sound at once, or, on another reading, a voice that drowns out a hundred others. The text then gives its uses: kept, it wards off evil; worn on the body, it cures jaundice.

Warding off evil is a stock virtue among the western beasts — the Tiangou and the Qitu of the same classic share it — but the Huan adds a medical power, and carries three marvels in one small body: the single eye, the triple tail, the hundred voices. Ming and Qing illustrators found it irresistible, usually drawing a cat-like creature with three erect tails and one eye set in the middle of its forehead. It shares Mount Yiwang with the Qitu, a three-headed, six-tailed bird that loves to laugh; between them, the final mountain of the guideway houses two of the strangest small creatures in the entire book.

value.en: A wildcat with one eye and three tails
As if a hundred voices sounded at once
Wards off evil; worn, it cures jaundice
